subject: english
topic_title: Speech Work: Stress in Words of Two Syllables
source_title: Third Term SS1 English Language Lesson Notes
learning_objectives: Identify two-syllable words stressed on the first syllable
Recognise noun-verb stress shift in two-syllable words

sections.heading: First-syllable Stress
sections.content: The note gives examples such as basic, solemn, credit, collar, Christian, certain, fortune, period, hatred, captain, and mansion.

sections.heading: Exceptions and Shifts
sections.content: Words with prefixes may take stress on the second syllable, as in begin, before, inform, away, include, and despite. Some two-syllable words take first-syllable stress as nouns and second-syllable stress as verbs, as in content, consent, import, and reform.
